content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcll PRACTTcBs C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 o 428 J Street r Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
May 21,2002
Richard L. Poland
333 West Broadway, Suite 200
Long Beach, CA 90802
Re
Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-02-044
Dear Mr. Poland:
This letter is in response to your request for advice regardingthe provisions of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1
You request reconsideration of the conclusion reached in the Poland Advice
Letter No. A-01-123, issued July 3, 2001, regarding advertisements you published in the
Pacific Bell Yellow Pages. In that letter, we concluded that a series of advertisements
you published in the Pacific Bell Yellow Pages constituted campaign expenditures under
the Act which should be paid from your campaign bank account. You request
reconsideration of that advice, or alternatively, request written assistance about how to
disclose the expenditures on your campaign reports.
You were a candidate for the office of Long Beach city prosecutor in an election
held April 9,2002, but were not elected to that office. The advertisement considered in
our initial advice letter was an ad in the Yellow Pages for your private law practice that
prominently contained your name "RICHARD L. POLAND, ATTORNEY AT LAW,"
and the words "voTE" and "oFFICE oF TI{E LONG BEACH CITY PROSECUTOR."
Prior to this advertisement, you ran another similar advertisement in the Grunio n Gazette
that omitted the word "VOTE." You believe that both advertisements were business
expenses and not campaign expenses, and are submitting this request for reconsideration.
Reviewing the advertisements in question and applicable law, we reaffirm the
conclusion reached in Advice Letter No. A-01-123 that these advertisements constitute
I Government Code sections 81000 - 91014. Commissionregulations appear at Title 2, sections
I 8 1 0 9 - I 8 997, of the California Code of Regulations.
r:=


File No. A-02-044
Page No. 2
campaign expenditures under the Act which must be paid from your campaign bank
account.
An "expenditure" is defined in section 82025 of the Act as follows:
"'Expenditure' means a payment, a forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a
loan by a third party, or an enforceable promise to make a payment, unless
it is clear from the surrounding circumstances that it is not made for
political purposes. . . ."
In addition, regulation 18225 defines an "expenditure" as:
"(a) An expenditure is any monetary or nonmonetary payment made for
political purposes. A payment is made for political purposes if it is:
(1) For the purpose of influencing or attempting to influence the action
of the voters for or against the nomination or election of a candidate or
candidates, or the qualification or passage of any measure; or
(2) Made by:
(A) A candidate, unless it is clear from surrounding circumstances that
the payment was made for personal purposes unrelated to his or her
candidacy or status as an offrce holder;
(B) A controlled committee...."
The advertisements you published in the Pacific Bell Yellow Pages and the
Grunion Gazette constitute campaign expenditures under section 82025 and regulation
18225.
In addition, section 85201 of the Act addresses the campaign bank account
"(a) Upon the filing of the statement of intention pursuant to Section
85200, the individual shall establish one campaign contribution account at
an offrce of a financial institution located in the state.
(b) As required by subdivision (f) of Section 84102, a candidate who
raises contributions of one thousand dollars ($1,000) or more in a calendar
year shall set forth the name and address of the financial institution where
the candidate has established a campaign contribution account and the
account number on the committee statement of organization filed pursuant
to Sections 84101 and 84103.
(c) All contributions or loans made to the candidate, to a person on
behalf of the candidate, or to the candidate's controlled committee shall be
deposited in the account.
(d) AnV personal funds which will be utilized to promote the election
of the candidate shall be deposited in the account prior to expenditure.
(e) All campaign expenditures shall be made from the account.
(f) Subdivisions (d) and (e) do not apply to a candidate's payment for a
filing fee and statement of qualifications from his or her personal funds.


File No. A-02-044
Page No. 3
(g) This section does not apply to a candidate who will not receive
contributions and who makes expenditures from personal funds of less
than one thousand dollars ($1,000) in a calendar year to support his or her
candidacy. For purposes of this section, a candidate's payment for a filing
fee and statement of qualifications shall not be included in calculating the
total expenditures made.
(h) An individual who raises contributions from others for his or her
campaign, but who raises or spends less than one thousand dollars
($1,000) in a calendar year, and does not qualify as a committee under
Section 82013, shall establish a campaign contribution account pursuant to
subdivision (a), but is not required to file a committee statement of
organization pursuant to Section 84101 or other statement of bank account
information.
Under section 85201(e) of the Act, all campaign expenditures are required to be
made from the campaign bank account.
You are also requesting written assistance as to how to correct any errors made in
your campaign reporting with respect to these ads. While we are providing assistance
with regard to amending your campaign reports, we note that amending the reports does
not erase any violation of the campaign provisions of the Act that may have taken place.
You may write a check to your campaign account for the amount of the Yellow
Pages and Grunion Gazette advertisements. You may then write a check from your
campaign committee to reimburse your business account from which you paid for the
ads.
Rather than amend your committee's previous filings which accurately reflect
what happened at the time, we recommend that you show the reimbursement discussed
above on your committee's next campaign statement due June 30,2002. You may report
the payment to your campaign account and the reimbursement on your committee's Form
460 as follows. Monetary contributions by candidates to their own committees are
reported on Form 460, Schedule A - Monetary Contributions Received. You may report
the payment to your committee to cover the cost of the advertisements on Form 460,
Schedule A. You would show this as one contribution on the date in 2002 when you
write the check to your campaign committee. On Schedule E - Payments Made, you may
report the payment to you or your business account, describing it as a reimbursement for
the phone book and newspaper advertisements. Thus, you may report the contribution
and reimbursement using one entry on Schedule A and one entry on Schedule E.


File No. A-02-044
Page No. 4
If you have any other questions regarding this matter, please contact me at (916)
322-s660
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
N1!. P' LJ
By: Hyla P. Wagner
Senior Counsel, Legal Division
HPW:jg
